Should the querist scratch the ground with his big toe, he
would be thinking of his fields. If he scratches his feet with the
hand, his thought concerns a maid servant.

If the querist looks at palm-leaves or birch bark, he must be
thinking of fine clothes ; if he stands on hair, husk, bone or ashes,

Science of Limbs LI 437
he will be attacked by disease ; and if he stands on, looks at or
holds ropes, nets or barks, while asking, he will suffer from
imprisonment.
